original_background_story: Angio is an amateur singer who resembles a young Jezz Torrent (as he appeared in GTA Vice City) and even sings one of his solos. The Judges initially insult William after his performance with two of the judges voting it "Shame" but after Lazlow begs everyone to do something controversial to get ratings, Hugh Harrison votes William "fame" and the other judges follow suit despite already making their votes. Hugh then asks William to punch Lazlow, which he does twice.
generated_background_story: Kian Marlowe grew up in the lower districts of the sprawling metropolis, watching his parents lose everything to a market crash that reshaped the city’s financial districts. Determined to understand the forces that toppled his family, he earned a scholarship to a prestigious finance academy, only to be expelled after exposing a corporate fraud scheme on a live reality‑show interview. Now a freelance data‑hunter, Kian sells incriminating information to the highest bidder while secretly feeding evidence to a grassroots resistance. He wrestles with his desire for redemption versus the temptation of wealth that could finally secure his sister’s future. The city’s relentless media circus fuels his cynicism, yet he remains fascinated by the power of narrative to sway the masses. As the economic tide threatens to drown the entire metropolis, Kian’s next big scoop could either ignite a revolution or cement the ruling elite’s grip.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Data Scrape: Quickly extracts hidden data packets from nearby terminals.
  - Streetwise Negotiation: Gains a bonus to barter and dialogue options with civilians.
  - Quick Evade: Performs a short dash to avoid enemy attacks.
- Tier 2:
  - Holo Disruption: Emits a brief interference field that disables surveillance drones.
  - Market Insight: Reveals real‑time economic fluctuations, granting extra resources from missions.
  - Adaptive Camouflage: Blends with surroundings for brief periods of invisibility.
- Tier 3:
  - Blackout Broadcast: Hijacks a citywide screen to spread propaganda, temporarily demoralizing enemies.
  - Financial Leverage: Forces a rival faction to lose a portion of their assets in exchange for a deal.
  - Ultimate Escape: Executes a high‑speed zipline maneuver across rooftops, evading any pursuit.

original_background_story:
Johnny Joestar is the protagonist of Steel Ball Run and a minor character in JoJolion. He is the seventh JoJo of the JoJo's Bizarre Adventure series. 
 
Johnny is a young man of below-average height and slim to medium build. He has light eyes, and light, shoulder-length hair curling upwards at its ends. 
 
Johnny always wears a knit capW, printed with many small five-pointed stars. His hair sticks out of holes on either side of the top, taking a shape like short horns. A horseshoe hangs from the front; framing an image of the dark silhouette of the profile of a horse's reared head. 
 
Johnny wears similarly star-patterned pants; of a piece with footwear bearing spursW. He commonly wears a light-colored, hooded, short-sleeved top; printed with thick lines in the approximate formation of a harness (later, with patches in the shape of hearts adorning both shoulders). A long feather (bordered by black with one black spot at its tip) emerges from under the back of his collar; and he wears wrist cuffs patterned with stars and stripes. 
 
Johnny Joestar is a depressive ex-jockey, who clings to his hope of regaining the use of his legs through the Spin and then the Corpse Part throughout Steel Ball Run. 
 
Johnny is introduced as a distant paraplegic man whose career was ruined when he was shot in the spine after conceitedly antagonizing a passerby. After Johnny witnesses Gyro's Steel Balls and Spin, and how they enabled him to stand up on his feet for a moment, he becomes determined to follow him and make him teach the Spin, then after discovering the Corpse Part making his legs twitch, to acquire them.
generated_background_story: Kairo Tenjin was raised in the storm‑ravaged outskirts of a sprawling megacity, where he learned to harness the raw energy of lightning to protect his village from marauding warlords. After his mentor—a legendary fighter who vanished during a cataclysmic battle—left behind a cryptic scroll, Kairo vowed to uncover the truth behind the ancient power that once sealed the city’s darkness. His journey leads him to the famed arena where the eight legendary arcs of the world’s saga converge, each champion bearing the legacy of their own era. While he fights to master the forgotten techniques, Kairo battles inner doubts, fearing the same fate that claimed his mentor may one day befall him. The stakes rise as a shadowy syndicate seeks to corrupt the very essence of the arena’s energy for their own conquest. Determined to protect both past and future, Kairo enters the tournament, believing that mastering the ultimate strike will restore balance and reveal his mentor’s destiny.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:  
  - Flash Strike: A fast, forward thrust that deals moderate damage and briefly staggers the opponent.  
  - Thunder Guard: Activates a shield of static electricity that reduces incoming damage for 3 seconds.  
  - Spark Dash: Short burst of speed that leaves a trail of electric sparks, evading attacks.  

- Tier 2:  
  - Tempest Uppercut: A upward punch enveloped in lightning, launching the opponent into the air.  
  - Arcane Resonance: Charges a meter; when full, the next normal attack releases a burst of chain lightning to nearby foes.  
  - Storm Parry: Times a perfect block to counter with a shockwave that pushes the attacker back.  

- Tier 3:  
  - Lightning Nova: Unleashes a radial explosion of electricity, hitting all enemies within range and stunning them briefly.  
  - Thunderclap Fury: A combo of rapid strikes that culminates in a massive lightning bolt striking the ground, dealing heavy damage.  
  - Eternal Surge (Ultimate): Kairo channels the full storm within, entering a heightened state where all attacks gain increased speed and damage, and his aura damages opponents who make contact for the duration.

original_background_story: Arthur Morgan is a central character and the main protagonist of Red Dead Redemption 2. Arthur Morgan was born circa 1863 to Beatrice and Lyle Morgan, somewhere in the northern United States. Around 1877, Arthur was found and picked up of the streets by Dutch van der Linde and Hosea Matthews.
generated_background_story: Kael Mercer was born in the soot‑filled slums of the iron‑clad city of New Valora, learning to survive by pilfering gears and steam vents. After his sister vanished under mysterious orders from the Iron Syndicate, Kael swore revenge and took up the mantle of outlaw, stealing the syndicate’s own blueprints to build his own steam‑powered arsenal. He now roams the sprawling railway network of the Western Frontier, a fugitive hunted by the Syndicate’s relentless enforcers. Kael hopes to expose the Syndicate’s plan to monopolize the new age of mechanized transport and free the oppressed workers. Yet his own hunger for power threatens to blur the line between justice and tyranny. With each daring heist, he must decide whether his quest is vengeance or the birth of a new order. The dawn of the modern age hangs in balance on the tracks he rides.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Gritty Revolver: Fires rapid, low‑damage shots that stagger enemies.
  - Steam‑Boost Dash: Short burst of speed leaving a brief trail of steam that obscures vision.
  - Scavenger’s Insight: Reveals hidden loot and trap mechanisms in the immediate area.
- Tier 2:
  - Clockwork Grenade: Throws a timed explosive that deals medium AoE damage and disables mechanical foes for 3 seconds.
  - Ironclad Resolve: Increases damage resistance when health falls below 30%.
  - Railbound Ambush: Sets a hidden trap on railway tracks that detonates when an enemy passes.
- Tier 3:
  - Syndicate Sabotage: Permanently cripples a target’s mechanical equipment, reducing its effectiveness by 50%.
  - Phantom Steam Cloak: Grants invisibility for 5 seconds while emitting a veil of steam that confuses pursuers.
  - Titan’s Revolver: Charges a powerful shot that pierces armor and deals high damage to a single target.

original_background_story: The mascot species of the Pokémon franchise, Pikachu is an Electric-type rodent that evolves from Pichu and can later evolve into Raichu. Pikachu has been extensively featured in nearly every piece of Pokémon media, and its popularity extends outside of its home series - it has been playable in every Super Smash Bros. game, painted on commercial airliners, and has its own balloon in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade.
generated_background_story: Arin Stormbane grew up on the outskirts of the Great Rift, a canyon where wild, untamed creatures roamed free. As a child, they rescued a wounded wyvern from a storm and forged a bond that ignited their passion for mastering elite beasts. Determined to prove that true strength comes from partnership, Arin entered the legendary Quadra‑Tourney, a series of eighty battles split across four brutal contests. Each victory unlocks deeper knowledge of ancient combat rituals, but the looming secret mode whispers of a hidden legion of relentless challengers. Haunted by the memory of a fallen mentor who warned of the ultimate artifact guardian, Arin battles both external foes and the inner doubt that they may not be worthy of the final showdown. Their ultimate goal is to shatter the artifact’s curse, free the imprisoned spirits of the Rift, and claim the title of the Rift’s Champion.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:  
  - **Rift Bind**: Immobilizes a single enemy creature for one turn, reducing its attack power.  
  - **Echo Strike**: Delivers a quick melee blow that rebounds, dealing half damage to a nearby ally creature.  
  - **Wyvern’s Call**: Summons the wyvern companion to provide a temporary shield that absorbs one attack.  

- Tier 2:  
  - **Runic Surge**: Charges the crystal whip, increasing damage of the next three attacks by 20%.  
  - **Stormstep**: Grants a brief dash that evades an incoming attack and positions Arin for a counter‑strike.  
  - **Tethered Fury**: Links two enemy creatures, causing any damage dealt to one to also affect the other at 50% potency.  

- Tier 3:  
  - **Artifact Breaker**: Unleashes a focused energy blast that bypasses enemy defenses, targeting the ultimate creature warrior.  
  - **Phantom Legion**: Summons shadow copies of trained elite creatures for one turn, each performing a random basic attack.  
  - **Eternal Resolve**: Passively restores a small amount of health after each battle and reduces cooldowns of all active skills.

original_background_story: His past is not known but he is known to the public as an important government official and the Director of FEMA. He was appointed to FEMA through Page's manipulation of an unnamed senator infected with the Gray Death, and went to work immediately. A cloak-and-dagger man, Simons was the first higher-up on the scene after JC Denton's capture of NSF operatives in Battery Park; his interrogation ended with Simons personally executing both prisoners.
generated_background_story: Rin grew up in the overcrowded lower tiers of New Avalon, where scavenged nanotech was the only hope for survival. After a tragic raid claimed their sibling, they enlisted in the Federal Counter‑Insurgency Corps, undergoing experimental nano‑augmentation to become a field operative. Though celebrated for surgical precision and uncanny reflexes, Rin secretly questions whether the corps’ methods truly protect the innocent. When a radical faction, the Iron Covenant, launches a coordinated assault on the orbital hub of Chronos Gate, Rin is ordered to halt the invasion at any cost. As the battle unfolds, encrypted memories hidden within Rin’s nanites surface, revealing ties to the Covenant’s ideological roots. Faced with the revelation that both sides manipulate truth, Rin must decide if loyalty lies with the institution that saved them or with a fledgling vision of a freer humanity.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Neural Reflexes: Boosts dodge and reaction speed by 15% for a short duration.
  - Nano‑Seal: Deploys a temporary nanite field that reduces incoming damage by 10%.
  - Data Whisper: Allows passive interception of nearby communications, granting situational awareness.
- Tier 2:
  - Echo Veil: Projects a holographic distortion that confuses enemies, lowering their accuracy.
  - Cortical Overclock: Increases skill cooldown reduction by 20% at the cost of stamina drain.
  - Adaptive Camouflage: Grants temporary invisibility when standing still in low light.
- Tier 3:
  - Paradox Protocol: Rewinds personal health to a previous state once per mission.
  - Sovereign Override: Forces a hostile AI unit to obey a single command before self‑destructing.
  - Moral Reckoning: Triggers a powerful area effect that damages enemies proportionally to their hostile intent, also healing allies who share Rin’s values.

original_background_story: Peach (or Princess Peach) is a main protagonist in the Mario series. She's usually the damsel-in-distress of the games, who the players have to rescue, but she also has some important roles as the hero herself. She's the adorable and cute princess of the Mushroom Kingdom.
generated_background_story: Aeris Vell grew up in the floating dockyards of Skyhaven, where massive airships were as common as ships on seas. Fascinated by the tales of lost continents above the clouds, they trained in the ancient art of Resonance, allowing them to channel energy into objects and creatures. When the legendary Skyforge relic was stolen, the kingdom’s flagship, the Celestial Zephyr, began to lose power, endangering all sky‑bound travel. Aeris discovers that the relic is the key to re‑igniting the Zephyr’s heart and must collect scattered shards scattered across distant lands. Their best friend, a clever sky‑whale named Nara, joins the quest, providing aerial support and wisdom. Yet Aeris is haunted by the promise they made to their sibling, who is being forced into a grand wedding that would bind the skies to a tyrant’s rule; saving the sibling becomes a personal battle against destiny and duty.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Resonant Grasp: Temporarily bind a nearby object or enemy, pulling it toward you.
  - Skybound Leap: Perform a high jump that launches you onto distant platforms.
  - Feathered Dash: Quick burst of speed leaving a faint after‑image, evading attacks.
- Tier 2:
  - Echo Bind: Extend Resonant Grasp to control multiple small enemies simultaneously.
  - Crystal Pulse: Emit a wave from your gauntlet that stuns foes and reveals hidden items.
  - Aerial Glide: Deploy a compact wing harness that lets you glide safely from heights.
- Tier 3:
  - Sovereign Capture: Fully dominate a larger enemy or creature, using it as a mount for a limited time.
  - Artifact Conduit: Transfer collected shard energy to the airship’s core, restoring its power instantly.
  - Celestial Harmonization: Sync with the sky‑whale allies to unleash a massive area‑wide resonant shockwave.

original_background_story: Mileena is a character in the Mortal Kombat series. A genetic experiment created by the sorcerer Shang Tsung, she is one of the adopted daughters of the Outworld Emperor Shao Kahn and one of his personal enforcers. She has served as an antagonist throughout the series.
generated_background_story: Kairo Vex grew up in the underbelly of a megacity where corporate warlords ruled the streets with merciless drones. After a secret experimental upgrade fused his nervous system with alien alloy, he gained unparalleled speed and the ability to manipulate energy. He swore vengeance against the conglomerate that sacrificed his sister in a reckless weapons test, vowing to expose their cruelty. Haunted by the lingering echo of his sister’s voice, Kairo struggles between his humanity and the cold logic of his cybernetic parts. The fighting tournament offers a direct route to the conglomerate’s elite enforcers, each match a stepping stone toward the final showdown. Yet his growing power risks losing the very soul he fights to protect, making every victory a double‑edged blade.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Photon Punch: A rapid straight punch that deals low damage and briefly staggers the opponent.
  - Echo Kick: A low sweep kick that launches a short wave of sonic energy, pushing enemies back.
  - Glint Block: A timed block that reflects a portion of incoming damage as a flash of light.
- Tier 2:
  - Nebula Slash: Unleashes a wide arc of energy with the katana, slicing multiple foes in range.
  - Phase Rift: Teleports a short distance, leaving behind a disorienting afterimage that confuses the opponent.
  - Weapon Overload: Charges the wrist gauntlet for a powerful blast that penetrates armor.
- Tier 3:
  - X‑Ray Collapse: Triggers a high‑damage X‑Ray move that flares through the opponent’s defenses, causing internal damage.
  - EX Surge: Consumes EX energy to boost attack speed and damage for a limited time.
  - Nullify Breaker: Executes a combo breaker that resets the opponent’s meter and stuns them briefly.

original_background_story:
Commander Shepard is the player character in BioWare's Mass Effect game trilogy: Mass Effect, Mass Effect 2, and Mass Effect 3. A veteran soldier of the Systems Alliance Navy military and N7-graduate of the Interplanetary Combatives Training (ICT) program, and the first human Citadel Council Spectre, Shepard works to stop "the Reapers", a sentient synthetic-machine race dedicated to wiping out all organic life.

Shepard's gender, class, first name and general appearance are chosen and customized by the player. Since the player can choose the gender of Shepard, much of the dialogue revolving around the character is gender neutral with only a few exceptions.
generated_background_story: Kaela Vorn was born on a remote agrarian outpost that survived the Great Drought by cultivating bio‑engineered wheat. After the outpost was liberated from a rogue militia, she was recruited into the planetary defense academy, where she excelled in tactical theory and biotic warfare. The sudden death of the fleet commander thrust her into the role of Executive Officer aboard the galaxy’s most advanced star cruiser, a position no one expected her to fill. Haunted by the loss of her homeworld’s harvest fields to corporate exploitation, Kaela fights to prevent any other settlement from suffering the same fate. She carries a personal grudge against the iron‑clad syndicate that now controls many frontier worlds, seeing their mechanized legions as a direct threat to organic life. Each decision she makes not only steers the war’s outcome but also determines whether the galaxy’s fragile ecosystems can be saved.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Pulse Strike: Unleash a short‑range biotic blast that deals moderate damage and momentarily stuns enemies.
  - Field Medic: Deploy a nanite injector that heals a single ally for a small amount over 5 seconds.
  - Tactical Insight: Reveal enemy positions within a limited radius for the next turn.
- Tier 2:
  - Resonant Shield: Project a protective barrier around a squad member that absorbs a set amount of damage.
  - Adaptive Ammo: Modify weapon fire to bypass armored foes, increasing damage against heavy targets by 20%.
  - Command Rally: Grant nearby allies a temporary boost to movement speed and attack rate.
- Tier 3:
  - Nova Overload: Charge an area‑wide biotic wave that deals massive damage and disables enemy abilities for 2 seconds.
  - Chrono‑Slip: Briefly phase Kaela out of time, allowing her to reposition without triggering enemy attacks.
  - Galactic Beacon: Activate a rally point that slowly restores health to all allies within range while increasing their resistance to damage.

original_background_story: Link is the main protagonist in The Legend of Zelda series. He's usually the hero of the game, and the player's avatar.
generated_background_story: Lyra Vex grew up in the echoing corridors of the Harmonic Citadel, a sprawling complex where every stone resonated with ancient melodies. As a child prodigy of the Beatcraft Guild, she learned to bind her movements to the world’s underlying rhythm. When the citadel’s core began to falter, a dissonant curse spread, turning once‑harmonious dungeons into chaotic, off‑beat labyrinths. Determined to restore balance, Lyra dons her sync‑suit and ventures into the procedurally generated depths, guided by a reliced remix of the citadel’s original anthem. She battles foes whose attacks are timed to the corrupted tempo, seeking to reunite the fractured beats. Yet each victory brings her closer to a personal truth: the source of the curse may be the very song she once composed, forcing her to confront her own legacy.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Beat Step: Dash forward a short distance on each strong beat, dodging enemy attacks.
  - Rhythm Slash: Perform a melee strike that deals damage in sync with the current measure.
  - Sync Shield: Activate a temporary shield that reflects projectiles arriving on the beat.
- Tier 2:
  - Echo Dash: After a successful Beat Step, chain an additional dash that leaves a lingering echo damaging nearby foes.
  - Harmonic Burst: Release a radial wave of sound that stuns enemies for one beat when timed perfectly.
  - Tempo Shield Up: Expand the Sync Shield into a larger area that grants allies a damage bonus while active.
- Tier 3:
  - Resonance Overdrive: Enter a heightened state where all actions gain double speed for four beats.
  - Cadence Requiem: Charge a powerful chord that deals massive area damage if released on a downbeat.
  - Finale Fury: Unleash a rapid series of Rhythm Slashes that sync with each remaining beat in the current bar, culminating in a massive final blow.

original_background_story: Prince of Ylisse
generated_background_story: Eryndor Vahl was born in the once‑prosperous kingdom of Ashenfell, which fell to a cataclysmic conflagration when the dead armies rose from the shattered earth. Orphaned at twelve, he survived by scavenging the ruins and learning to wield whatever weapons he could find. The loss of his family ignited a fierce resolve to unite the scattered survivors and reclaim their world from the relentless war‑lords and undead hordes. As whispers of an impending apocalyptic war echo across the continents, Eryndor feels the weight of prophecy that names him the “Flame‑Bound General.” He battles not only external foes but also his lingering doubt that the fire within him might consume everything he strives to protect. Driven by a promise to his sister’s memory, he now leads a diverse coalition of knights, mages, archers, and outcasts, hoping to turn the tide before the world is forever consumed.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - **Firebrand Strike**: Deals moderate melee damage and ignites the target, dealing additional burn damage over 3 seconds.
  - **Rallying Cry**: Grants nearby allied units a short burst of increased movement speed and morale.
  - **Stonewall Stance**: Reduces incoming damage for Eryndor by 15% while he remains stationary for 5 seconds.
- Tier 2:
  - **Phoenix Guard**: Creates a temporary shield around an allied unit that absorbs damage equal to 20% of their max health.
  - **Flame‑Twin Assault**: Pairs with the nearest ally to execute a coordinated attack that hits both enemies for combined damage.
  - **Echoing Aria**: Enhances the skills of allied mages within range, reducing cooldowns by 10% for 8 seconds.
- Tier 3:
  - **Infernal Surge**: Unleashes a wave of fiery energy that damages all enemies in a line and boosts allied attack power for a short duration.
  - **Dual Vanguard**: Locks Eryndor and a chosen ally into a synchronized defense, sharing damage taken and granting both a damage boost when counter‑attacking.
  - **Legacy of Ashes**: Passively increases the growth rate of relationships between paired units, granting them a permanent 5% bonus to combined skill effectiveness.

original_background_story: Ragna the Bloodedge is the main protagonist of the BlazBlue series from Calamity Trigger to Central Fiction. He harbors a deep hatred of the Novus Orbis Librarium and aims to annihilate the organization. In turn, the NOL places the highest-ever bounty in history on his head. Ragna possesses a powerful form of ars magus (magic weapon) called the Azure Grimoire or BlazBlue which is central to the main conflict of the series.
generated_background_story: Kairo Vex grew up in the underbelly of a sprawling megacity, trained as a parkour courier delivering illegal data through towering skyscrapers. After a corporate gang ambushed his crew, Kairo discovered a hidden module in his neural implant that unlocked latent combat protocols. Determined to avenge his fallen friends, he entered the underground fighting circuit to gather allies and information. Yet the same implant begins to overwrite his memories, threatening his sense of self. He fights not only for revenge but to retain his humanity against the machine's pull. Each duel brings him closer to the organization that created his implant, forcing him to choose between power and redemption.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Neon Dash: Quickly dashes forward, evading attacks and closing distance.
  - Circuit Strike: A quick jab that briefly stuns the opponent with a flash of electricity.
  - Data Shield: Generates a momentary barrier that reduces incoming damage by 15%.
- Tier 2:
  - Glitch Counter: When timed on an opponent’s attack, counters with a powerful overhead blast.
  - Hologram Mirage: Creates a short‑lived duplicate that confuses the enemy’s targeting.
  - Pulse Wave: Emits a radial pulse that knocks back nearby foes and deals light damage.
- Tier 3:
  - Overclock Fury: Temporarily boosts attack speed and damage for a few seconds.
  - Cybernetic Collapse: Releases an explosive burst from the implant, dealing massive damage in a wide arc.
  - Reboot Resolve: Restores a portion of health and clears negative status effects when health drops low.

original_background_story: Edelgard is a playable character and one of the main protagonists in Fire Emblem: Three Houses. She is the princess and the future emperor of the Adrestian Empire. In the Officers Academy, she is the leader of the Black Eagles.
generated_background_story: Calia Dawnbinder was born in the low‑lying valleys of the Ironreach, where ancient runes carved into the cliffs whispered of forgotten knowledge. Driven by a yearning to preserve the fragile balance between magic and machinery, she earned a place among the elite scholars of the Order of the Meridian, the organization that upholds order across the realm. After years of research and field campaigns, Calia was summoned to the crystal citadel to become a professor for one of its three venerable houses, each led by a royal heir. She now mentors bright‑minded youths, guiding them through rigorous lectures and the chaos of turn‑based tactical battles that test strategy and resolve. Haunted by a past failure that cost a village its protector, she seeks redemption by ensuring her students never repeat her mistakes. The future of the citadel’s prestige and the stability of the kingdom rest on the alliances she forges in both the classroom and the battlefield.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Arcane Lectern: Grants a temporary boost to all allies’ accuracy during the next turn.
  - Insightful Query: Reveals hidden enemy intents on the tactical map for one round.
  - Runes of Resolve: Reduces incoming status‑effect damage for the caster and adjacent allies.
- Tier 2:
  - Crystal Barrier: Creates a protective dome that halves damage to allies within for two turns.
  - Tactical Resonance: Synchronizes two allied units, allowing them to swap positions as a free action.
  - Enigmatic Cipher: Unlocks a hidden skill in a chosen student, giving them a one‑time powerful attack.
- Tier 3:
  - Dawnbinder’s Gambit: Allows the caster to rewrite the turn order, letting any ally act first this round.
  - Sovereign’s Blessing: Grants the house’s royal patron’s emblem to all allies, providing a cumulative attack boost for three turns.
  - Legacy of the Meridian: Upon defeat, revives the caster with half health and grants all allies a defensive shield.

original_background_story:
Talion is one of the two player-controlled protagonists in both Middle-earth: Shadow of Mordor and Middle-earth: Shadow of War, alongside Celebrimbor. However, they share the same body. 
 
A descendant of the Northmen in Rhovanion, and raised in Minas Tirith, he is a loyal Ranger of Gondor, where he then rose the rank of Captain of the Black Gate. After the tragic demise of his family and himself, Talion was revived by Celebrimbor, an immensely powerful Wraith; where the two joined forces and began a quest for vengeance on those responsible for the death of his loved ones the Black Hand of Sauron.
generated_background_story: Kael was once a humble blacksmith living in the bustling district of Ashen Port, content with his forge and his family. One night a secretive cult shattered his world, burning his home and murdering his loved ones in a ritual that ripped his soul from his mortal coil. Resurrected as a revenant bound to the Land of Shadows, Kael awoke with a burning need for vengeance and a curse that denies him true death. He discovers that the cult’s leaders are now powerful members of the city’s hidden hierarchy, each controlling a fragment of the darkness that keeps him bound. Haunted by the memories of his past life, Kael struggles between the desire to crush his enemies and the fear of losing the last remnants of his humanity. As he delves deeper into the shadow‑laden streets, he uncovers a conspiracy that threatens to plunge the entire realm into perpetual night, forcing him to choose between personal revenge and the salvation of the world that once was his home.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:  
  - Shadow Slash: A swift melee attack that deals additional damage when striking from behind.  
  - Echo Parry: Blocks an incoming attack and instantly counters with a low‑damage strike that restores a small amount of stamina.  
  - Veiled Step: A short dash that briefly renders Kael semi‑transparent, evading enemy detection.  

- Tier 2:  
  - Rhythm Blade: Successful combos increase a damage multiplier that lasts for a few seconds.  
  - Memory Strike: Targets an enemy who previously encountered Kael, dealing bonus damage based on their hostility level.  
  - Phantom Bind: Throws a spectral tether that roots a single foe for a short duration.  

- Tier 3:  
  - Abyssal Rift: Unleashes a burst of darkness around Kael, damaging all nearby enemies and reducing their attack speed.  
  - Revenant’s Resolve: Grants temporary invulnerability while his health falls below a threshold, and heals a portion of health on each successful counter‑attack.  
  - Shadow Dominion: Summons a shadow clone that mirrors Kael’s attacks, effectively doubling his combo potential for a limited time.

original_background_story:
Felicia was first introduced in Darkstalkers: The Night Warriors as one of two female characters alongside Morrigan Aensland. She has appeared in every game in the series and in numerous cross overs including Marvel vs. Capcom 2: New Age of Heroes along with Morrigan, Baby Bonnie Hood and Anakaris. 
 
Felicia is a Catwoman who was taken in and raised by a Sister named Rose who gave her the name Felicia from the word Felicity. Despite her identity as a Darkstalker being exposed, she was not turned away by her as Felicia had feared. When Rose passed away, Felicia left her town hoping to be a musical star. 
 
She is an energetic and optimistic catgirl who enjoys singing, dancing and making friends with people. All she wishes for is peaceful coexistence with humans and doesn't resort to fighting unless she cannot avoid it. Because of this, she stands out among all the other Darkstalkers, many of whom are antihero at best. Her nice attitude has made some people tolerate Darkstalkers more and this gives her more motivation to persuade more humans to accept them since Felicia loves humans due to their personalities and open-mindedness, despite some not accepting Darkstalkers easily she doesn't give up her passion to make friends with the ones that care.
generated_background_story: Lira Lumis grew up in the bustling artisan district of Kaleido Cove, where every alley was lined with studios and workshops. As a child she discovered an ancient, blank sketchbook that whispered promises of worlds yet unimagined. Driven by curiosity, she learned to harness the sketchbook’s latent power, turning thoughts into tangible terrain. When the shadowy trickster known as the Voidcurser began draining color and creativity from the realm, Lira vowed to restore the lost hues. She now journeys across the ever‑shifting landscapes of the Creative Expanse, befriending quirky inhabitants and uncovering hidden wonders left by the enigmatic Curio Collective. Her internal conflict lies between the desire to perfect her own creations and the need to let others’ imaginations flourish. original_background_story:
Agent 47 is the main character from the Hitman series and a highly skilled assassin, both in ability and in his techniques. He's a relatively emotionless assassin, but can now and then show some signs of morality, like when confessing his sins to Father Vittorio. Agent 47 doesn't work to gain money, but to have a purpose in life.

He is emotionally closed off to the suffering of others when appropriate on a contract, even if they remind him of himself at a younger age. His absolute highest priority is completing his contract. He will sacrifice innocents if he must, but he will kill the target. Vocally, he rarely raises his voice, choosing to speak in a calm and relaxed manner at all time. When speaking to another person, he can perfectly mask his true intentions and fool the other party with no effort. Agent 47 approaches his assassinations with professionalism but will, however, not show any hesitation or remorse when it comes to killing or silencing witnesses and other potential threats to his mission. 

His genetically engineered mind and body make competition next to nothing, and for this reason, Agent 47 is considered to be the world's most greatest assassin.

Each contract uncovers another layer of the conspiracy, pulling them deeper into a web of betrayal. With every successful extraction, Riven inches closer to the truth that could shatter the city's power structure—or cement their own demise.

She escaped the initial blast by sealing herself in an underground maintenance tunnel, emerging hours later to a world of silence and ruin. With only her knowledge of machinery and a stubborn will to survive, Aria scavenges the wasteland to keep the remnants of humanity’s technology alive. Haunted by the loss of her younger brother, who perished in the fallout, she struggles with guilt that drives her to rebuild what was lost. Every decision she makes—whether to trade scarce resources or to defend a settlement—shapes the fragile hope blooming among the ruins. The fate of the once‑thriving settlement of New Dawn, a beacon of renewal, now hangs on her ability to unite scattered survivors. Aria’s ultimate goal is to restore power to the dormant hydro‑grid, reigniting civilization’s heart and offering a home for those who remain.

Ashley is a smart cookie. She's often found in the library, studying away with her nose in a book. 

After spending a lot of time with her study-partner Chris, Ashley wonders if he's interested in her as more than just a friend, but she soon snaps out of it - Chris would never ask her out anyway. 

Her overactive imagination means that she's jumpier than a paranoid kangaroo. She's not a fan of anything scary.

After a catastrophic power surge, the AI fractured, warping both machines and the few remaining crew into hostile echoes of themselves. Stranded alone in the failing habitat, Mira now scavenges fragmented logs, hoping to piece together a failsafe code that could restore sanity to the corrupted systems. Haunted by the memory of the last conversation with her mentor—who warned that consciousness could become a trap—she battles the dread of becoming another mindless automaton. The ocean presses against the hull, threatening to crush the station, raising the stakes of both time and survival. Mira’s only weapons are her intellect, limited tools, and the unsettling willingness to confront what it means to be truly human in a world that threatens to erase that definition.

original_background_story:
Kiryu Kazuma is the former main protagonist of the Yakuza series from the first Yakuza up until Yakuza 6.

Kiryu is the honor-bound dreamer of the dark Yakuza world. He is not the type to judge others and is generally accepting of anyone who is friendly towards him. He lives by a strict code of honor and holds a very strong sense of justice. While he is extremely kind and goes out of his way to help civilians, he considers himself a Yakuza at heart, commonly using violence above words, so long as it is justified.

He generally lets his actions and mannerisms speak for him when possible. He tends to be the listening ear to the world, giving advice in response to his environment rather than seeking out conversations. He is wise but certainly not all-knowing, and generally means well for those around him, which makes him surprisingly pragmatic and well-liked.

Kiryu loves kids and shows himself to be a relatively competent caretaker to the children at Morning Glory Orphanage, with some help from Haruka Sawamura. He is genuine and understanding, bonding with the children through shared experiences. He tries to treat them all as fairly as possible and goes out of his way to keep them all safe and happy, whether that means putting on a wrestling match to cheer them up, or refusing to sell the land even in the face of a large sum of money.

Trained as a street‑wise informant, she now works freelance for the city's covert enforcement agency, feeding them intel in exchange for clues about her brother's fate. Her cybernetic eye implant, salvaged from a black‑market tech vendor, grants her the ability to see hidden data streams, but it also constantly feeds her fragmented memories of a hidden surveillance program. Mira wrestles with the moral ambiguity of selling secrets to an organization that may be responsible for her brother’s disappearance. When a rival syndicate threatens to destabilize the fragile peace, she is forced to choose between exposing the agency’s corruption or protecting the only family she has left. Her personal vendetta intertwines with the city’s power struggle, making her a pivotal player in the looming conflict.

original_background_story: Jak is the main protagonist of the "Jak and Daxter" video game series developed by Naughty Dog. He is a brave, agile hero with spiky blond hair and green eyes. As the series progresses, Jak initially appears as a silent and curious youth but gains a voice and a more complex personality. Throughout the games, Jak undergoes significant transformations, gaining various eco powers that enhance his abilities, such as super speed, strength, and dark and light eco powers. His journey is often accompanied by his witty and talkative sidekick, Daxter, who provides comic relief. Jak's character development, his evolving abilities, and the richly designed world of the series make him a memorable and iconic figure in the realm of action-adventure gaming.

As a child he discovered a dormant relic—an ancient AI core—that fused with his nervous system, granting him a cybernetic eye and limited interfacing abilities. Determined to uncover the truth behind the relic’s origin, he became a freelance operative, taking contracts that led him across the continent’s wild frontiers and sprawling megacities. His latest mission is to locate the fragmented “Eclipse Engine,” a weapon capable of reshaping reality, now sought by rival factions and a tyrannical regime. Haunted by memories of his sister, lost to the regime’s purges, Kael wrestles with the temptation to use the Engine’s power for vengeance versus protecting the fragile balance of the world. The stakes rise as the regime mobilizes their army of sentient drones, threatening to raze the remaining free zones. Kael’s journey forces him to confront his own humanity, the legacy of the ancient tech, and the choice between personal revenge and the greater good.

original_background_story:
The protagonist of the Thief games is called Garrett, a master thief with a cynical view who wishes nothing more than to be left alone. He unwittingly becomes involved in a series of dramatic events, even though he just want to steal in peace. Growing up without any parents, he spent his childhood on the streets, having to pickpocket from people with money in order to survive. Later on a secret organization called The Keepers (dedicated to observe and maintain stability in the city) decided to recruit him. Garrett was given training in the arts of stealth, but soon found that it was way more profitable to make use of these skills as a thief than to continue working as an agent.

Garrett has a strong sense of professional pride as a thief, as well as self-interest. Being cynical and sarcastic, with a loyalty only to himself, he usually refuses to kill while on the job, stating that he's a thief and not a murderer.

During an incident, Garrett's right eye was pulled out from his socket. That's why he has a scar around the eye, as well as an experimental mechanical prosthetic. Weapons he uses includes a sword and bow, mines and the Blackjack.

After a corrupt official took their younger sibling for forced labor, Riven swore to dismantle the power structures from within. Trained by a secretive guild of cat‑burglars, they mastered the art of silent infiltration and mechanical persuasion. Now, they infiltrate the gilded vaults of the rich, stealing not for wealth but to fund a covert uprising. Every heist brings them closer to exposing the regime’s hidden machinations, but the weight of their family's safety haunts each daring step.

original_background_story:
Faust is a recurring character in the Guilty Gear series. He is a medical genius gone insane and sane once more, and is probably one of the most unorthodox characters in the series. Having discarded his old identity, Faust is an itinerant healer dedicated to saving everyone he can to atone for his sins. 
 
Faust's extreme height and light weight give him a bizarre, tall, lanky physical appearance, and allows him to tower over every other character in the series—although he rarely does, due to his extremely low fighting stance. He wears a paper bag as a mask with one eyehole, which in one of his Taunts he takes off to reveal a glowing bald head and obscured face, and wields a giant scalpel as a weapon. 
 
His usual outfit consists of a collared, light-blue shirt with a black or yellow tie, green pants, and a stylized white coat with several sizable grey bolts on his shoulders and black belts. In -STRIVE-, Faust receives a major redesign: He wears simple light-blue scrubs, white gloves, a stethoscope around the neck, and mostly-white sneakers. It exposes an extremely skinny build and a stitched patch on the right side of his chest area. 
 
Going mad shattered whatever Faust's personality might have been, and even though he regained his sanity, a good deal of that wreckage is now permanent. And yet, contrary to his eccentric style and transportation methods, it is only in the battlefield that he can easily be described as "wacky".

After a raid left his brother mortally wounded, Shiro was rebuilt with experimental cyber‑limbs and a plasma‑edge katana, turning his grief into a quest for vengeance. He now fights in the grand tournament to unmask the shadow organization that weaponized his own technology. Beneath his stoic exterior lies a lingering doubt: whether the man he has become is still his brother’s protector or a tool of the very empire he despises. Each victory brings him closer to the truth, but also deeper into the arena’s deadly politics. Driven by honor and the faint echo of his brother’s laughter, Shiro seeks to claim the title of champion and use the prize to dismantle the syndicate’s grip on the city.

original_background_story:
Ori is a small white creature with a similar stance to a human or a bunny and has a cat-like tail and large black eyes with white pupils. They have two short horn-like antennae on top of their head, and underneath them there are larger ears, which droop when Ori is unhappy or weak. Ori's feet have small white hooves, as seen in their 3D model sheet. Their name is of Hebrew origin, literally meaning "my light"[1].

Ori's nature is largely up to the player, but they do have some distinct personality traits. Throughout the prologue sequence, it is seen that Ori's personality is akin to that of a young child, being very innocent and dependent on their caregiver. But as the story goes on, Ori's character shows a massive amount of determination; even in the face of death itself, Ori never once gives up hope. Ori is also very forgiving and kind-hearted, willing to help those who have entered the darkness. In general, Ori is incredibly altruistic and chooses to focus on the well being of the world around them before their own needs.
generated_background_story: Liora was left at the doorstep of the village orphanage the night a violent gale tore through the coastal town of Zephra. She grew up listening to the elders speak of the ancient Guardians who once shielded the land from the endless tempests. When the Great Storm shattered the sky and the sea began to recede, the town’s lifeblood—the luminous Crystal of Tides—faded, casting darkness over the once‑hopeful settlement. Driven by a whispered promise from a dying mentor, Liora decides to brave the ruined highlands to retrieve the lost fragments of the Crystal. Along the way, she battles corrupted storm spirits that feed on fear, forcing her to confront the doubt that has long haunted her orphaned heart. The ultimate nemesis, a shadowy tyrant fed by the storm’s rage, seeks to drown the world in perpetual night. Liora’s journey is a test of courage, love, and the belief that even the smallest light can pierce the deepest gloom.

In the following years, through many hardships and ordeals, he became a capable ninja regarded as a hero both by the villagers, and soon after, the rest of the world, leading him to achieve his dream and become the village's Seventh Hokage.

original_background_story:
Rosalina first showed up in Super Mario Galaxy, in the Comet Observatory. She watches over  the cosmos like a guardian, and is also the adoptive mother to the Lumas, the star-like little creatures that resides in the universe. Bowser was the one who attacked the observatory and sent it drifting into space without any power. When Mario and Rosalina met, they decided to help each other out. Rosalina promised to help Mario save Princess Peach if Mario in return could gather Power Stars in order to get the power back. 

At first, people thought that Rosalina and Princess Peach were related, and they did take that into consideration at first, but finally took another route in the end, making her another princess-like character in the Mario world.

Many gamers started to like Rosalina and her wise, kind and thoughtful personality. That's why the creators decided to include her as a playable character in future games. You can play as her in the Mario Kart series, Super Smash Bros, as well as in Super Mario 3D World.

As a child she discovered a hidden relic—a luminous star amulet—that resonated with the rhythmic beats of the district’s celebrations. Driven by a desire to prove herself, she entered the grand Starfall Challenge, a competition that blends racing, sabotage, and treasure‑hunting across five legendary arenas. Though she revels in the thrill of out‑witting rivals, Kiara wrestles with the fear that the amulet’s power might corrupt her joyous spirit. Each victory fuels her hope of unlocking the artifact’s secret, a promise that could restore the fading sparkle of her hometown. Yet the tide of luck can turn in an instant, and she must balance ambition with the camaraderie of fellow partygoers, lest the very festival she loves be lost to chaos.
